success_surface: Multi-condition row filtering with safe cell updates and post-execution verification
effective_behaviors: Pre-execution sheet inspection to understand structure and data types
Type-safe numeric validation for detecting whole numbers across integer and float representations
Two-phase workflow: inspection/dry-run before actual data modification
Post-execution verification by re-reading updated cells to confirm changes applied correctly
Comprehensive logging of affected rows and operation summary for auditability
Safe file handling: write to new output path rather than overwriting source file
Skip header row assumption with configurable start position
Graceful error handling for non-numeric or missing values in conditional columns
evidence: Agent printed spreadsheet structure (sheet names, max_row, max_column) before processing
Used try/except block around float() conversion to handle non-numeric values gracefully
Implemented whole number check via num_val == int(num_val) to catch both int and float types
Performed verification loop after save to re-read updated cells at rows 10 and 13
Tracked updated_rows list and update_count for summary reporting
Saved to separate output_path instead of modifying wb_path directly
Started iteration from range(2, ws.max_row + 1) assuming row 1 is header

memory_items.title: Pre-Execution Sheet Inspection
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect worksheet structure, dimensions, and sample data before implementing modifications
memory_items.generalization: Before writing data transformation code, programmatically load the workbook, list sheet names, print dimensions (max_row, max_column), display header row contents, and sample a few data rows to verify assumptions about column positions and data types.

memory_items.title: Type-Safe Whole Number Detection
memory_items.lesson: When checking if a value is a whole number, account for both integer and floating-point representations
memory_items.generalization: Use float() conversion followed by comparison (num_val == int(num_val)) to detect whole numbers. Wrap in try/except for ValueError/TypeError to safely skip non-numeric values like None, strings, or corrupted entries.

memory_items.title: Multi-Condition Row Filtering Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Combine multiple column-based conditions using nested IF logic during row iteration
memory_items.generalization: For conditional updates spanning multiple columns, iterate through each row and evaluate all conditions sequentially before applying changes. Track matching rows in a list for logging and potential rollback capabilities.

memory_items.title: Post-Modification Verification Protocol
memory_items.lesson: After saving modifications, re-read and log the changed cells to confirm accuracy
memory_items.generalization: Following wb.save(), read back the modified cells at known row indices to verify the updates were applied correctly. Report verification results alongside the execution summary.

memory_items.title: Safe File Output Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Never overwrite the source spreadsheet; always write modifications to a new file path
memory_items.generalization: Create output directory if needed, then save modified workbook to a distinct filename/path. This preserves the original data and allows easy comparison between input and output states.

edits.content:
## 6. Conditional Updates & Validation
- **Multi-Column Conditions**: When filtering rows based on conditions across multiple columns, iterate through data rows and evaluate all conditions before applying updates.
- **Whole Number Detection**: Use `float()` conversion followed by `num_val == int(num_val)` comparison to detect whole numbers from both integer and float representations. Wrap in try/except to handle non-numeric values.
- **Pre-Modification Inspection**: Before executing data changes, print worksheet structure (sheet names, dimensions, headers, sample rows) to verify column positions and data type assumptions.
- **Post-Update Verification**: After saving modifications, re-read and report the changed cells at specific row indices to confirm updates were applied correctly.
- **Safe Output Handling**: Always save modified workbooks to a new file path rather than overwriting the source file. Create output directory if it doesn't exist.
